t-20260219-001.title: [Forum] Critical: Global: we need an "are you sure you want to close" popup confirmation before closing any popup input like the
t-20260219-001.description: Critical: Global: we need an "are you sure you want to close" popup confirmation before closing any popup input like the feedback input.

agentConfig.response:
Syntax check passes. Here's the summary:

## Changes Applied

### Files Modified
- `static/js/forum.js` — Added close confirmation guards for edit mode, navigation, and Escape key

### What Was Done
Added "are you sure?" confirmation popups before closing any input that has unsaved content:

1. **Edit mode cancel** — `cancelEditMode()` now checks if the title or content was modified before discarding. If changes exist, user must confirm.
2. **Navigation away from edit mode** — The hashchange handler now guards against navigating away from a post while in edit mode with unsaved changes.
3. **Escape key handler** — New global Escape key listener that dismisses popups in priority order: emoji picker (no confirm needed), edit mode (confirms if changes), reply forms (confirms if has content).

The existing guards were already in place for: new post form cancel, navigating away from new post form, navigating away with unsaved comments, reply form cancel/toggle. The feedback modal in the shared navbar already had its own `guardedClose()`.

### Remaining Items
- Service restart needed (`opai-forumbot`) to serve updated static files (if the server caches them)
- The shared feedback modal (in `navbar.js`) already has close confirmation — no changes needed there

t-20260219-002.title: [Forum] owners of form posts need to be able to esit the posts when on their post, place an edit button at the top of the screen
t-20260219-002.description: owners of form posts need to be able to esit the posts when on their post, place an edit button at the top of the screen when they are on their own post. if they click it they can simply click on areas like the title or body of the post to edit them. click the save button (edit button transformed the moment the edit mode was entered) to save, place a Cancel button next to the edit button once clicked that canceled and discards all edits.

agentConfig.response:
## Changes Applied

### Files Modified
- `static/js/forum.js` — Added edit mode state management, edit/save/cancel functions, edit button bar in post detail view, exposed methods on `forumApp`, added `SVG_EDIT` icon, gave title and body elements IDs + `data-raw-content` attribute for raw markdown storage
- `static/css/forum.css` — Added styles for `.edit-btn-bar` (show/hide logic via `.editing` class), `.editable-active` outline highlight, monospace styling for content editing

### What Was Done
When a user views their own post, an **Edit Post** button now appears above the post card. Clicking it:
1. Transforms the button bar to show **Save** and **Cancel** buttons (Edit disappears)
2. Makes the title (`<h2>`) and body (`<div class="post-body">`) contenteditable with a purple outline indicator
3. The body switches from rendered markdown to raw text for editing
4. **Save** calls `PUT /api/posts/{id}` (existing endpoint, already checks `author_id`) and re-renders the post
5. **Cancel** discards all edits and restores the original title/content with re-rendered markdown

### Remaining Items
- The `opai-forum` service needs a restart for static file cache to clear (if any caching is in place) — a human should restart it
- Admins who are also post owners will see the Edit button (this is correct behavior — admins can always edit their own posts via the owner check in the API)

t-20260219-007.description: the re-submit button on the All Tasks tab needs to be on the Audit tab soo for each report and resubmit the task to the queue

t-20260220-001.title: opai-portal: feedback-queue.json write is not thread-safe (concurrent requests can lose data)
t-20260220-001.description: In tools/opai-portal/app.py around line 240, the /api/feedback and /api/request endpoints both perform a read-modify-write on feedback-queue.json with no file locking or mutex. If two users submit feedback simultaneously, the second write will clobber the first, silently dropping a feedback entry. The comment says 'Atomic-ish write' but there is no actual atomicity. Fix: add a threading.Lock() around the read-append-write block, or use append-only writes (one JSON object per line). Also applies to the /api/request endpoint which writes to the same file.

t-20260220-002.title: opai-portal: _feedback_rate and _request_rate dicts grow unbounded (memory leak)
t-20260220-002.description: In tools/opai-portal/app.py, _feedback_rate and _request_rate are module-level defaultdict objects that accumulate one entry per unique client IP address and are never pruned. On a long-running server with many unique callers the dicts will grow indefinitely. Old entries whose timestamp windows have expired are only cleaned on the NEXT request from the same IP. Fix: periodically expire old IP entries (e.g. in a background task or by capping the dict size), or switch to a TTL cache like cachetools.TTLCache.

t-20260220-003.title: opai-portal: task ID generation has race condition when multiple requests arrive simultaneously
t-20260220-003.description: In tools/opai-portal/app.py /api/request handler (around line 320), the portal reads registry.json, counts existing tasks for today, derives a next_num, and writes the new task — all without a lock. The opai-tasks service does the same in services.py generate_task_id(). If both services run concurrently (common: portal submits a user request at the same time the tasks auto-executor creates a task), they can generate the same t-YYYYMMDD-NNN ID and one write will overwrite the other. Fix: use the services.generate_task_id() function exclusively through the tasks API, or add a cross-process file lock (e.g. fcntl.flock) around registry reads and writes.

t-20260220-004.title: opai-chat: /api/files/read and /api/files/search do not enforce sandbox scope for non-admin users
t-20260220-004.description: In tools/opai-chat/routes_api.py, the /api/files/browse endpoint correctly scopes non-admin users to their sandbox path (config.USERS_ROOT / user.id). However /api/files/read (line ~160) and /api/files/search (line ~175) pass the caller-supplied path directly to context_resolver.resolver without applying the same sandbox restriction. A non-admin user can supply any allowed-root path (e.g. /workspace/synced/opai/notes/) and read or search files they should not access. The context_resolver does check ALLOWED_ROOTS and BLOCKED_PATTERNS but does not enforce per-user sandbox isolation. Fix: apply the same user_root scoping guard to read_file and search_files that browse_files already uses.
t-20260220-004.cancelReason: Tool opai-chat has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-005.title: opai-chat: CORS wildcard origin combined with allow_credentials=True is a security misconfiguration
t-20260220-005.description: In tools/opai-chat/app.py the CORS middleware is configured with allow_origins=['*'] AND allow_credentials=True. This combination is invalid per the CORS spec (browsers reject credentialed requests with a wildcard origin) and could allow cross-site request forgery from any origin if a browser bug or non-browser client is used. FastAPI/Starlette will actually raise an error at runtime about this combination. Fix: replace allow_origins=['*'] with an explicit allowlist of permitted origins (e.g. ['https://opai.boutabyte.com', 'https://opai-server']) and keep allow_credentials=True.
t-20260220-005.cancelReason: Tool opai-chat has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-006.title: opai-chat: conversation ID generation uses Python object id() which is not unique across processes or restarts
t-20260220-006.description: In tools/opai-chat/conversation_store.py create_conversation(), the ID is built as conv_{timestamp}_{id(timestamp) % 10000:04x}. id() returns the memory address of the Python object, which resets every restart and can collide across the 4-hex-digit (65536-value) range if two conversations are created in the same second. On a busy server this is a real collision risk that would silently overwrite an existing conversation file. Fix: replace id(timestamp) with uuid.uuid4().hex[:8] or secrets.token_hex(4) for a random, collision-resistant suffix.
t-20260220-006.cancelReason: Tool opai-chat has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-007.title: opai-monitor: /api/system/stats and /api/system/services expose system metrics with no authentication
t-20260220-007.description: In tools/opai-monitor/routes_api.py, the GET /api/system/stats and GET /api/system/services endpoints have no auth dependency. The comment on system_stats says 'No auth — same as /health/summary' but /health/summary is intended to be a machine-to-machine check whereas /api/system/services returns a list of all running systemd services, their states, and the ability to infer the server topology. The /api/agents endpoint and /api/agents/{pid} are also unauthenticated, exposing running PID data and process command lines. The WebSocket endpoints /ws/stats, /ws/agents, /ws/logs all accept connections with no authentication. Fix: add require_admin dependency to /api/system/services, /api/agents, /api/agents/{pid}, and implement token-based auth on the WebSocket endpoints before streaming.
t-20260220-007.cancelReason: Tool opai-monitor has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-008.title: opai-tasks: write_registry() is not protected by _registry_lock — concurrent threads can corrupt registry.json
t-20260220-008.description: In tools/opai-tasks/services.py, read_registry() and write_registry() both lack the _registry_lock mutex. The auto_execute_cycle acquires _registry_lock only for the initial read (line 65374), then immediately releases it. Meanwhile run_agent_task(), _run_feedback_fix(), and the HTTP API route handlers all call write_registry() outside any lock. If the auto-executor launches a task in a thread that calls write_registry() at the same time an API request does, the last writer wins and earlier writes are lost — potentially marking a task as pending again when it should be in_progress, or dropping status changes. Fix: acquire _registry_lock in all write_registry() call sites, or make read_registry/write_registry manage the lock internally.
t-20260220-008.cancelReason: Tool opai-tasks has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-009.title: opai-tasks: GET /api/tasks and GET /api/tasks/{task_id} have no authentication — any unauthenticated caller can read all task data
t-20260220-009.description: In tools/opai-tasks/routes_api.py, the list_tasks and get_task endpoints have no auth dependency. All other mutating endpoints (POST, PATCH, DELETE) correctly use Depends(require_admin), but the read endpoints are open. The task registry may contain sensitive information including user emails from feedback submissions, agent instructions, source references, and project/client names. Fix: add Depends(require_admin) to list_tasks and get_task, or at minimum add Depends(get_current_user) to require a valid Supabase session.
t-20260220-009.cancelReason: Tool opai-tasks has been deleted in v3.5

t-20260220-010.title: opai-wordpress: /api/backups/{backup_id}/download does not verify the requesting user owns the backup
t-20260220-010.description: In tools/opai-wordpress/routes_automation.py, the download_backup endpoint fetches the backup from Supabase by backup_id only, with no user_id filter. Any authenticated user who guesses or brute-forces a UUID backup_id can download another user's site backup, which includes full database exports and may contain sensitive customer data. Similarly, list_backups in the same file queries wp_backups filtered only by site_id with no user_id guard, allowing a user to enumerate another user's backups if they know the site_id. Fix: add &user_id=eq.{user.id} filter to the backup lookup in download_backup (and allow admins to bypass), and add the same guard to list_backups.
t-20260220-010.notes: Still relevant - security fix needed. WordPress backup download endpoint lacks user ownership check.

t-20260220-011.title: opai-wordpress: app.py health endpoint reports version '1.0.0' but FastAPI app declares version '1.5.0'
t-20260220-011.description: In tools/opai-wordpress/app.py, the FastAPI app is initialized with version='1.5.0' but the /health endpoint hardcodes 'version': '1.0.0'. The monitor health aggregator uses the version field from health responses, so the monitor will always display an outdated version for the WordPress tool. This also indicates the health endpoint was not updated when the tool was upgraded. Fix: replace the hardcoded '1.0.0' string in the health() function with a module-level VERSION constant that matches the FastAPI app definition, or read it from config.py.

t-20260220-012.title: opai-wordpress: deprecated @app.on_event('startup') — migrate to lifespan context manager
t-20260220-012.description: In tools/opai-wordpress/app.py, the startup logic uses the deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup') decorator. This was deprecated in FastAPI 0.93 and will be removed in a future version. It also means the background tasks (update_checker, scheduler_loop, connection_agent_loop) started inside the handler are not properly cancelled on shutdown, potentially leaving zombie asyncio tasks. Fix: migrate to the lifespan context manager pattern (already used correctly in opai-monitor and opai-tasks) using @asynccontextmanager and the lifespan= parameter on the FastAPI constructor, which allows proper startup and graceful shutdown of all background tasks.

t-20260222-005.title: [replace_line] Projects/SEO-GEO-Automator/Codebase/webapp/package.json
t-20260222-005.description:
**Source:** Evolution Plan (evolve_safe_plan.md)

**File:** Projects/SEO-GEO-Automator/Codebase/webapp/package.json
**Action:** replace_line
**Reason:** dep_scanner.md (Finding M-06), report_dispatcher.md (DEP-M06). axios is a runtime HTTP client used in production application code. Listing it under devDependencies means it is absent from production installs (`npm ci --production` / `npm install --omit=dev`), causing a runtime crash when the production build tries to import it. In a Vite project the bundle is identical regardless of section — this change only fixes the production install scenario.

```fix
FILE: Projects/SEO-GEO-Automator/Codebase/webapp/package.json
LINE: 14-15
ACTION: replace_line
REASON: dep_scanner.md (Finding M-06), report_dispatcher.md (DEP-M06). axios is a runtime HTTP client used in production application code. Listing it under devDependencies means it is absent from production installs (`npm ci --production` / `npm install --omit=dev`), causing a runtime crash when the production build tries to import it. In a Vite project the bundle is identical regardless of section — this change only fixes the production install scenario.
BEFORE:     "react": "^19.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.0"
AFTER:     "axios": "^1.13.2",
    "react": "^19.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.0"
```

t-20260223-001.title: Fix: orchestrator email-check failed
t-20260223-001.description:
Auto-generated from AI audit analysis.

## Audit Context
- **Audit ID**: audit-20260223-431-077312
- **Service**: opai-orchestrator
- **Event**: email-check
- **Agent**: ?
- **Status**: failed

## AI Analysis
# OPAI Log Analysis: Email Check Failure

## Status
**🔴 FAILED**

---

## What Happened
A system-level email check task was triggered by the orchestrator at 19:31:17 UTC on 2026-02-23. The operation timed out after ~33 seconds and failed to complete. This is a non-AI service task (token counts = 0), likely part of the email agent's scheduled polling routine.

---

## Issues Found

| Issue | Severity | Detail |
|-------|----------|--------|
| **No error details** | HIGH | Summary only says "Email check failed — 33s" with no exception trace |
| **Missing metadata** | MEDIUM | Agent, Agent Type, Model all unknown (?) — suggests incomplete logging |
| **Unclear failure mode** | HIGH | Could be: IMAP timeout, auth failure, network issue, or service offline |
| **32.6s duration** | MEDIUM | Long for email poll; may indicate network lag or hanging connection |

---

## Suggested Fix

**Investigate the actual error:**

1. **Check orchestrator logs** for the specific exception:
   ```bash
   tail -n 100 /workspace/synced/opai/logs/orchestrator.log | grep "email-check"
   ```

2. **Check email agent service status:**
   ```bash
   ./scripts/opai-control.sh status | grep email-agent
   ```

3. **Review email agent logs** (if available):
   ```bash
   tail -n 50 tools/opai-email-agent/data/agent-state.json
   ```

4. **Common causes to check:**
   - IMAP server connectivity (email credentials, firewall, ISP blocking)
   - Email agent service crash or hung process
   - Supabase/database connection timeout
   - Rate limiting on email account

**Create a task if needed:**
```
Title: Debug email-check orchestrator task failure (audit-20260223-431-077312)
Description: Email check hung at 33s with no error trace. Investigate orchestrator logs for IMAP/auth/network failure. Check email-agent service status.
Priority: Medium
```

t-20260226-001.title: [TeamHub] On the main List page in the dashboard when you filter it only allows you to type in a single letter and then the curser
t-20260226-001.description: On the main List page in the dashboard when you filter it only allows you to type in a single letter and then the curser jumps out of the search block and sorts the page. This happens with every letter you type in the filter box

agentConfig.response:
## Changes Applied

- **`tools/opai-team-hub/static/app.js`** — Fixed the filter input focus-loss bug on the List dashboard.

**Root cause:** `renderHomeListTable()` was replacing `el.innerHTML` with the entire page HTML (header + filters + table) every time the search changed. This destroyed and recreated the `<input>` element, immediately stealing its focus after each keypress — so only one character at a time could be typed before the cursor jumped out.

**Fix:**
1. Extracted row-building logic into a new `buildHomeListRows(items)` helper function.
2. Added a surgical-update path to `renderHomeListTable()`: if `.home-list-view` already exists in the container, only update the subtitle text (item count) and the `tbody` innerHTML directly — leaving the `<input>` element untouched and in focus.
3. The full `innerHTML` render only runs on the initial paint when `.home-list-view` doesn't yet exist.

No service restart required — this is a static JS file change that takes effect on browser reload.

t-20260301-test1.title: Test fleet dispatch: Research top 3 OPAI resilience improvements
t-20260301-test1.description: This is a test task for the fleet coordinator. Research and identify the top 3 improvements that would make OPAI more resilient. Consider: service recovery, data persistence, monitoring gaps, and single points of failure. Write a brief report with findings.

prop-1772491693-project-.title: [Proposed] opai-portal: add integration test for concurrent feedback submissions

t-20260303-001.title: [Files] the File system for Me (Denise) is not showing up, something about the sandbox not being available
t-20260303-001.description: the File system for Me (Denise) is not showing up, something about the sandbox not being available

agentConfig.response:
## Changes Applied

- **`tools/opai-files/app.py`** — Fixed the `_get_user_root` helper: instead of immediately raising a 503 when Denise's sandbox directory doesn't exist on disk, the code now calls `root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` to auto-create it. A 503 is still raised (with a clearer message) only if the OS-level `mkdir` itself fails, and a separate 503 path remains for the edge case where the path exists but isn't a directory. This means first-time or newly-provisioned users will get their sandbox directory created on first access rather than seeing "Sandbox directory not available."

> **Note for humans:** A service restart of `opai-files` is required for the change to take effect. Run `./scripts/opai-control.sh restart opai-files` (or equivalent).

**Agent**: self_assessment
**Squad**: evolve

**Critical/High findings**: 10

### P0 — Do Now
- Fix executor_safe apply pipeline — increase max_turns from 15 to 30 in `scheduler.py:495`. The entire daily safe-fix automation loop is broken.
- Fix WordPress backup download auth bypass (`t-20260220-010`) — `/api/backups/{backup_id}/download` does not verify ownership. 12 days unaddressed. **ACTION REQUIRED**
- Investigate and fix security agent output — `security.md` is 384 bytes (should be 10-30KB). Security audit is effectively non-functional.

### P1 — This Sprint
- Increase fleet worker max_turns — project-builder (25→40), project-reviewer (15→25). 7/11 recent dispatches wasted.
- Add queue.json dispatch mechanism — 9 items (2 critical) with zero completions ever. Queue is write-only.
- Add failed-task retry logic — 3 tasks stale in "failed" for 8+ days with no retry.
- Resolve forumbot worker health — 48 consecutive unhealthy cycles, no alert generated.
- Fix config.py port mismatch — PORT=8080 vs orchestrator.json api.port=3737.

### P2 — Backlog
- Clean phantom schedule entries from engine-state.json (self_assessment, evolution).
- Fix email agent lastCheck null state tracking.
- Remove test-nas-01 test worker from NFS dispatcher state.
